title: dinebra decipiens subsp. peacockii
intro: Dinebra decipiens subsp. peacockii, also known as the Peacock's Tail Grass, is a perennial plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Dinebra decipiens subsp. peacockii include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in traditional medicine, and it is typically made into a tea or infused oil for topical application.
